effaffir. After ! had been talking
with Governor Gooding, native
Of Boise turned to me and said,
pointing to the governor:


Hes a nervy proposition. They
will get him the same as they did
Steuenberg. It's only a question
of time, and he knows ii as well
as anybody"


You mean that he is marked
for assassination? asked in
some surprise.


NO doubt Of It," the speaker re-
piled at may be soon, and it
may be years, but they'll get him.


BOISE CITY. Idaho, Sept. IS
Politics in Idaho is a strange mix
up of Mormonism and labor trou-
bles. Republicanism and democ-
racy are but names.


The question whether United
States Senator Fred Dubois will
be returned to the senate is one
of those to be determined at this
falls elections. Another matter is
the fate of the new anti-Mormon
party, which Senator Dubois has
been chiefly instrumental in Great
ing.


And third, and quite as import
ant question, Is that involved in the
effort of organized labor to secure
the release and vindication Of
Messrs. Meyer and Haywood. lead
ing officers of the Western Fedora
ton of Miners, who stand charged
with the terrible murder by a
bomb of former Governor Steuen-
berg of this state.


The state is rent with factiOnal-
ism and the people are very bitter.


The anti-Mormon or, as it has
been named. the American party, is
really the creation OF Senator Fred
Dubois. The latter was first sent
~ IHe Culltu STATES senate as d
republican about IS years ago. In
the rise of silver as an issue in
1896. Dubois became convert to
16 to /, bolted Mckinley and re-
tired to private life.


Five years ago he was returned
to the senate by a ombined vote
Of the democrats, silver republicans
and pOpulists. But now silver is
nowhere and Dubois is without a
party.


Confronted with this situation,
and with return to private life
again staring him in the face, the
astute Idaho senator attempted a
bold stroke. He saw his state
made up of two-thirds gentiles and
one-third Mormons. He figured that
If he could raise the religious Issue
he could remain on top. Hence
arose the American party.


The game was good one and
promised well for Dubois until it
